process_name: Flavor-changing neutral-current top decay t -> u gamma
implementation_difficulty_reason: Cataloging the limit is straightforward, but production integration needs a new electroweak dipole/top-FCNC operator convention, RS-to-top-flavor matching for t-u rotations, and a t -> u gamma decay-width or production/decay recast path not present in the current Delta F = 2 and mu -> e gamma code.

evidence: Required plan greps rerun on 2026-05-16 across quarkConstraints/, qcd/, flavorConstraints/, neutrinos/, yukawa/, warpConfig/, solvers/, scanParams/, and tests/.
The implemented hits are the existing neutral-meson Delta F = 2 and mu -> e gamma paths; no top FCNC photon observable, tqgamma Wilson coefficient, or t -> u gamma branching-ratio check was found.
Targeted grep for t -> u gamma / tu gamma / tq gamma / top-photon FCNC returned no matches in the inspected code paths.
grep_commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints/ qcd/ flavorConstraints/ neutrinos/ yukawa/ warpConfig/ solvers/ scanParams/ tests/ -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints/ qcd/ flavorConstraints/ neutrinos/ yukawa/ warpConfig/ solvers/ scanParams/ tests/ -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "MODERN_PHENOMENOLOGY_SYSTEM_IDS|DEFAULT_DELTA_F2_INPUTS|evaluate_bd_mixing|evaluate_bs_mixing|evaluate_d0_mixing|check_mu_to_e_gamma" quarkConstraints/ qcd/ flavorConstraints/ neutrinos/ yukawa/ warpConfig/ solvers/ scanParams/ tests/ -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "t.?->.?u.?gamma|t.?->.?gamma.?u|tu.?gamma|tq.?gamma|FCNC.*gamma|top.*photon|photon.*top|B\\(t.*u.*gamma\\)|BR\\(t.*u.*gamma\\)" quarkConstraints/ qcd/ flavorConstraints/ neutrinos/ yukawa/ warpConfig/ solvers/ scanParams/ tests/ -g "!*.ipynb"

paper_era_reference.role: Warped-flavor reference epoch for post-2008 comparison.
Theory/projection context only, not a measured observable.
values: SM B(t -> c gamma) is order 1e-14; up-quark modes are about one order lower.
HL-LHC t -> uZ / u gamma sensitivity from Zt/gamma t production: 1e-5 level.
FCC-hh t -> uZ / u gamma sensitivity: 1e-6 level.

status_history.reason: Initial PKA draft and minimal source snapshot deposit.
Writer polish for wa_w7_new_processes; template sections normalized and numerical claims checked against sidecar/source snapshots.

issues: TeX dataset claims 139 fb^-1 and 138 fb^-1 are stored in auxiliary_experimental_context, not pdg_or_equivalent.
TeX future-sensitivity claims at 1e-5 and 1e-6 level are stored only under paper_era_reference, not pdg_or_equivalent.

status_history.reason: Addressed CA CHK-1 by removing the flagged luminosity and projection-reach numbers from TeX.
Cycle-2 checker pass completed; CHK-1 through CHK-8 passed.

status_history.notes: Fetched cited PDG/ATLAS/CMS/arXiv sources; tugamma value claims match.

status_history.reason: Opus round 3 sign-off; full PKA -> WA -> CA cycle-2 -> fact-check VERIFIED; see flavor_catalog/signoff/round_003_index.md.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(\mathcal{B}(t\to u\gamma)\).
This entry catalogs the flavor-changing neutral-current top decay
\(t\to u\gamma\), process id T004 in the top/Higgs/electroweak family.  It
covers direct searches for an anomalous photon coupling between a top quark and
an up quark, including FCNC single-top-plus-photon production and \(t\bar t\)
events with one FCNC top decay.
sections.pdg_prose:
The \texttt{pdg\_or\_equivalent} block in \texttt{T004.yaml} records both the
PDG combined row and flavor-separated primary limits.  The PDG 2026 top-quark
listing gives a combined
\(t\to\gamma q\), \(q=u,c\), branching-fraction limit
\[
  \mathcal{B}(t\to\gamma q) < 9.5\times 10^{-6}
\]
at 95\% CL (\texttt{PDG2026:T004:tgammaq\_combined}).  For the
flavor-separated \(t\to u\gamma\) mode, the tightest primary limit found is the
ATLAS result:
\[
  \mathcal{B}(t\to u\gamma) < 0.85\times 10^{-5}
\]
for the left-handed \(tq\gamma\) benchmark and
\(\mathcal{B}(t\to u\gamma)<1.2\times10^{-5}\) for the right-handed benchmark,
both at 95\% CL (\texttt{ATLAS2023:T004:tugamma\_left},
\texttt{ATLAS2023:T004:tugamma\_right}).  CMS later reported
\(\mathcal{B}(t\to u\gamma)<0.95\times10^{-5}\), with
\(1.20\times10^{-5}\) expected, at 95\% CL
(\texttt{CMS2024:T004:tugamma\_observed}).  Local snapshots are under
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/T004/}.
sections.relevance:
In warped or partially composite flavor models, non-universal fermion profiles
and mass-basis rotations can induce top FCNC couplings.  The photon channel is
a dipole-like probe, complementary to \(t\to uZ\), \(t\to ug\), and \(t\to uh\).
Compared with charm final states, T004 isolates the top-up flavor rotation and
therefore probes a different corner of the anarchic up-sector structure.
sections.post_2008:
The comparison baseline is arXiv:0804.1954
(\texttt{CFW2008:T004:rs\_flavor\_context}).  Since that reference epoch,
the important change is experimental: ATLAS and CMS set direct mode-resolved
limits (\texttt{ATLAS2023:T004:dataset}; \texttt{CMS2024:T004:dataset}).  The
high-energy single-production strategy also became a standard way to discuss
future sensitivity for \(t\to uZ/u\gamma\)-equivalent interactions.
sections.validity:
Experimentally this is a clean null-search upper limit; the SM rate is far
below current reach and is lower than the corresponding \(t\to c\gamma\) rate
because of CKM suppression
(\texttt{AguilarSaavedra2017:T004:SM\_and\_projection\_context}).  The
interpretation is not model-independent: ATLAS quotes separate chiral
\(tq\gamma\) tensor benchmarks, and an RS backend must choose an operator
normalization before comparing scan points to the branching-fraction limit.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
Coverage is \textbf{NO}.  The PKA reran the required catalog greps across
\texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/}, \texttt{flavorConstraints/},
\texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/}, \texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/},
\texttt{scanParams/}, and \texttt{tests/}.  The targeted \(t\to u\gamma\),
\(tu\gamma\), \(tq\gamma\), and top-photon FCNC search returned no observable,
Wilson coefficient, or branching-ratio implementation.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
Implementation difficulty is \textbf{HIGH}.  The repo has no top-FCNC photon
dipole basis, no RS-to-\(tu\gamma\) matching convention, and no top radiative
decay-width or production-recast module.  A production backend would need those
ingredients before this limit could become a scan cut.
